message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Wally's Desert Turtle (Rancho Mirage)
Cuisine/type signal: Continental / fine dining
Why it surfaced in discovery: Opened 1978 by Wally Botello (founder of Velvet Turtle); one of the original anchors of Rancho Mirage 'restaurant row'; longtime landmark on Palm Springs Life 'oldest restaurants' list.
Discovery sources: https://www.palmspringslife.com/the-oldest-restaurants-in-the-desert/ | https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/blog/post/historic-restaurants-in-greater-palm-springs/ | https://www.opentable.com/features/historic-restaurants-palm-springs-ca

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

Based on the search results, here's information about Wally's Desert Turtle in Rancho Mirage:

## Restaurant Overview
Wally's Desert Turtle is a culinary haven where classic elegance meets approachable modern sophistication, serving traditional California cuisine reimagined with a contemporary twist.

## History and Ownership
Wally's Desert Turtle was established in 1978 by Mr. Wally Botello, the founder of the famed fine-dining Velvet Turtle chain. The restaurant is now owned and operated by his son Michael and his wife Nicole, with an extensive menu served by classically trained Executive Chef Richard Pelz.

## Location and Operations
The restaurant is located at 71775 Highway 111, Rancho Mirage, CA 92270. The restaurant is seasonal and closes for the summer, reopening in October.

## Menu Highlights
Menu standouts include sautéed Dover sole, braised short ribs, Icelandic salmon, and roasted rack of lamb. The restaurant is famous for its soufflés, including Valrhona Chocolate, Grand Marnier, Bananas Foster, and Raspberry varieties.

## 2026 Events
Wally's Desert Turtle participates in Greater Palm Springs Restaurant Week with two specially curated 3-course dinner menus.

## Recognition
The restaurant is rated 4.6 stars by 4266 OpenTable diners.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Wally's Desert Turtle reviews on Google and Yelp:

## Yelp Reviews
Wally's is known for its Imported Dover Sole, Rack Of Lamb and decadent Soufflés, and has been open since 1978 and in the same family for three generations. The restaurant is located at 71775 Highway 111, Rancho Mirage, CA 92270, and has 468 photos and 515 reviews on Yelp.

## Overall Reception
Reviewers describe Wally's Desert Turtle as "the ultimate in enjoying a nostalgic fine dining experience" with the classic rat pack vibe, luxurious decor, amazing service and exquisite food.

The restaurant offers a "beautiful restaurant" experience with "outstanding service and food," and guests rave about the "flawless" service, "perfectly cooked" dishes, and "amazing" soufflés.

## Notable Awards and Recognition
The restaurant is the proud recipient of the prestigious Mobil Travel Four Star Award, AAA Four Diamond Award, and the DiRoNA Award.

The reviews across platforms consistently praise the elegant ambiance, fine dining experience, and classic dishes, though some reviews mention high prices.

content.content:
# Wally's Desert Turtle - Restaurant Summary

**Rating & Ranking**
Wally's Desert Turtle holds a 4.3 out of 5 stars based on 357 reviews and ranks #3 among 80 restaurants in Rancho Mirage.

**Location & Contact**
The establishment is situated at "71775 Highway 111, Rancho Mirage" and operates as a women-owned fine dining venue. You can reach them at +1 760-568-9321.

**Cuisine & Atmosphere**
Specializing in American steakhouse fare, the restaurant emphasizes "farm to table" practices. Reviewers consistently praise its "old world charm" and vintage Hollywood glamour ambiance, enhanced by live jazz performances.

**Hours**
The restaurant opens at 5:30 PM Tuesday-Thursday, and 5:00 PM Friday-Saturday. Mondays are closed, with limited Sunday hours (5:30-8:30 PM).

**Signature Items**
Diners frequently highlight the Grand Marnier soufflé dessert as exceptional. Popular entrees include Dover sole, rack of lamb, and scallops.

**Guest Experience**
Recent reviews (2025) praise exceptional service and curated menus. However, some note occasional wait times despite reservations and pricing at the higher end of the market range.

Based on the search results, here are the best fine dining restaurants in Rancho Mirage for 2026:

## Top Fine Dining Recommendations

**The Edge Steakhouse**
Located within The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage, The Edge crafts remarkable wagyu steaks, both premium hand-cut and bone-in meats as well as seafood paired with an international wine list unrivaled in California's Greater Palm Springs Valley. The restaurant is open Monday through Sunday from 5:00 p.m. to 10 p.m.

**Wally's Desert Turtle**
Established in 1978 by renowned restaurateur Wally Botello, Wally's Desert Turtle is a significant part of Rancho Mirage's culinary history and is celebrated as the pioneer of fine dining in the desert, offering a top-notch experience. It offers a refined, unhurried dining experience rooted in classic desert style, and under third-generation owner Madalyn Botello, thoughtful updates include curated wines, nightly entertainment and the Sahara Lounge, with polished service, elegant California cuisine and signature soufflés.

**Willie's Modern Fare**
Restaurateur Willie Rhine seamlessly pairs five-star hospitality with the elegance of fine dining, offering classic cuisine with a modern twist, complemented by inventive cocktails and a thoughtfully curated wine list. Guests can treat themselves to traditional afternoon tea on Sundays.

**Foxy's Kitchen + Bar**
One of the newer names on Rancho Mirage's dining scene, Foxy's Kitchen + Bar is a family-run spot where steaks and osso buco are favorites, complemented by an array of seafood, pastas, and vegan options.

**Si Bon**
Si Bon is a culinary gem that offers a delightful fusion of French and Belgian cuisine with a charming and inviting atmosphere, featuring delectable menu items with classic French and Belgian dishes, expertly prepared with a touch of creativity and flair.

Based on the search results, here are some of the most notable historic restaurants in the Coachella Valley and Palm Springs area:

## Long-Standing Classics

Las Casuelas, serving delicious iconic Mexican food since 1958, was the first Mexican restaurant in Palm Springs and the oldest restaurant in Palm Springs. For over six decades, the Delgado family has been passionately creating timeless and authentic Mexican food.

Keedy's, a vintage-styled diner just off Highway 111, has been a popular spot for breakfast and lunch since 1957, and family owned for many years, it still has that homey feel.

Sherman's Deli & Bakery was established in 1963 by Sherman Harris, who hailing from New York, brought the flavors of a traditional Jewish deli to the desert.

## Upscale Dining Establishments

Le Vallauris is a historic home built in 1927 that was later converted for the restaurant, and Le Vallauris opened in 1973.

The Nest is a welcoming upscale establishment with 55 years of history behind it and is the place to be for those who want to have a great dinner and be able to groove to live music.

Copley's on Palm Canyon Drive is housed in actor Cary Grant's former estate, where elegant fare like pan-seared jumbo scallops with white truffle and braised Berkshire pork belly are served with stunning views of the San Jacinto Mountains.

Based on the web search results, here are some excellent romantic date night restaurants in Rancho Mirage:

## Top Recommendations

**Wally's Desert Turtle** - Founded in 1978 by acclaimed restaurateur Wally Botello, this restaurant features a stellar menu crafted by French culinary expert Jean-Louis Jalouneix and an elegant, classical interior. You can enjoy a meal in one of the cozy booths to experience the restaurant's candlelit ambiance, twinkling chandeliers and hand-painted murals.

**Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine** - This restaurant offers a romantic and authentic Mediterranean dining experience, with trickling water features and a lush atrium interior that transports guests to the bluffs of the Mediterranean Sea. The menu features Spanish and Italian favorites including artisanal salads, starters, charcuterie and main courses.

**Pinzimini** - Nestled within the Westin Mission Hills in Rancho Mirage, Pinzimini showcases Tuscan-style Italian cuisine and has become a go-to spot for both locals and visitors since its inception in 2012.

**Shame on the Moon** - This unique restaurant is located in a refurbished airplane hangar with an extensive menu of American cuisine and is a great choice for a romantic dinner.

**Ristorante Mamma Gina** - This local favorite is known for its classic Italian cuisine and its cozy and intimate atmosphere.

These restaurants offer a variety of cuisines and atmospheres perfect for a romantic evening out in Rancho Mirage.

Links: [{"title":"About — WALLY'S DESERT TURTLE","url":"https://www.wallysdesertturtle.com/about"},{"title":"WALLY’S DESERT TURTLE - Updated May 2026 - 468 Photos & 515 Reviews - 71775 Highway 111, Rancho Mirage, California - Steakhouses - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/wallys-desert-turtle-rancho-mirage"},{"title":"Wally’s Desert Turtle Offers the Best in Fine Dining in the Desert","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/wallys-desert-turtle-rancho-mirage-california/"},{"title":"Madalyn Botello - Owner and Operator - Wally's Desert Turtle | LinkedIn","url":"https://www.linkedin.com/in/madalyn-botello/?_l=en"},{"title":"Inspiring Conversations with Madalyn Botello of Wally's Desert Turtle - Voyage LA Magazine | LA City Guide","url":"https://voyagela.com/interview/inspiring-conversations-with-madalyn-botello-of-wallys-desert-turtle/"},{"title":"Wallys Desert Turtle - Rancho Mirage Californian Restaurants and Bars","url":"https://directory.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/wallys-desert-turtle/"},{"title":"Desert Jewel | ICONIC LIFE","url":"https://iconiclife.com/desert-jewel/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Wally's Desert Turtle and Madalyn Botello:

Madalyn Botello is the third generation owner of Wally's Desert Turtle, following her grandfather Wally Botello who founded the restaurant. She grew up working at the Rancho Mirage restaurant that her grandfather, Wally, founded in 1978.

Botello is a Cornell University hospitality school alumna who has brought fresh perspectives to the restaurant, including boutique wines, elevated outdoor experiences, and creative additions to the nightly live music. After Wally's passing in 1985, his son Michael Botello (Madalyn's father) took over and nurtured the restaurant into a treasured hidden gem.

Wally's Desert Turtle is an award-winning fine dining restaurant in Rancho Mirage renowned for its elegant ambiance, exceptional service, exquisite California cuisine, timeless décor, and locally sourced ingredients.

Links: [{"title":"Desert Dining Suggestions - Sheri Dettman","url":"https://yourresorthome.com/desert-dining-suggestions/"},{"title":"13 Best Continental Restaurants In Palm Springs, Palm Springs / Palm Desert Cities | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/cuisine/best-continental-restaurants-palm-springs-ca"},{"title":"The Legends of Fine Dining | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/the-legends-of-fine-dining/"},{"title":"Palm Springs Restaurants in Historic Buildings | Where to Dine","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/palm-springs-restaurants-in-iconic-buildings/"},{"title":"Palm Springs Restaurant, Modern European Gourmet Cuisine Johannes","url":"https://johannespalmsprings.com/"},{"title":"Copley's on Palm Canyon","url":"https://www.copleyspalmsprings.com/"},{"title":"Best Continental Restaurants in Palm Springs / Palm Desert Cities | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/cuisine-in-metro/palm-springs-restaurants/continental"},{"title":"Best Restaurants in Palm Springs - Palm Springs","url":"https://www.palmsprings.com/restaurants/"},{"title":"130 Best American Restaurants In Palm Springs | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/cuisine/best-american-restaurants-palm-springs-ca"}]

Based on the search results, here are some excellent options for old-school fine dining with continental cuisine in Palm Springs:

## Classic Continental Fine Dining Establishments

**Jillian's Fine Dining**
Jillian's Fine Dining offers old school fine dining in a classic 1940's bungalow.

**Melvyn's Restaurant**
Tucked away in the gardens of The Ingleside Inn — the legendary, 80-year-old hotel — is Melvyn's Restaurant, which features a menu of fine continental cuisine and two world-class wine cellars serving four distinct dining rooms. Melvyn's became one of Palm Springs' legendary supper clubs, associated with names like Frank Sinatra and the Rat Pack era, and stays true to that legacy with classic continental cuisine executed with confidence, featuring tableside Caesar salad, prime rib, Dover sole, and perfectly timed martinis.

**Le Vallauris**
Le Vallauris opened in 1974 in a Robertson house built in 1924 by the son of Palm Springs pioneer Nellie Coffman. The menu reflects classic French technique with continental flair: duck, filet, lobster, soufflés, rich sauces, and a formal old-world dining style that feels increasingly rare.

**Johannes**
Johannes has been voted "Best Continental Restaurant" and "Best Fine Dining" by Palm Springs Life Readers. The menu features Austrian and Central European style dishes that are seldom seen, even in continental restaurants.

These establishments offer the classic, elegant fine dining experience with traditional continental cuisine that defines old-school Palm Springs dining.

input.name: Wally's Desert Turtle
input.cuisine_or_type: Continental / California fine dining (classic, soufflé-forward, jacket-era room)
input.corridor: Highway 111 corridor, Rancho Mirage (the original Rancho Mirage 'restaurant row' anchor, near The River)

reputation.review_volume: Yelp 515 reviews; Tripadvisor 357 reviews (#3 of 80 in Rancho Mirage); OpenTable cited as 4.6 across ~4,266 diner reviews. Google review count [UNVERIFIED] — not directly fetched.
reputation.sentiment: Mostly positive — strong recurring praise for service, nostalgia/Rat Pack ambiance, Dover sole, rack of lamb, and soufflés; minority of reviews note high prices and occasional pacing/wait issues.
reputation.recency_signal: Recent 2025-2026 reviews continue to praise service polish, curated menus, live music in the Sahara Lounge, and the third-generation refresh under Madalyn Botello; restaurant is seasonal and reopens October 9, 2026.
reputation.longevity: Operating since 1978 — 48 years. Continuous family ownership: founder Wally Botello, then son Michael Botello (after Wally's passing in 1985), and now third-generation owner Madalyn Botello (Cornell hospitality alumna).
credentials: Wine Spectator Award of Excellence (current as of 2025)
AAA Four Diamond Award (long-running; site/press claim continuous since 1993 — [UNVERIFIED] for the start-year specifically)
DiRoNA Award (Distinguished Restaurants of North America) — cited in press [UNVERIFIED] for current status
Mobil Travel Four Star Award — cited in press [UNVERIFIED] for current/historic status
Featured on Palm Springs Life 'oldest restaurants' / 'legends of fine dining' lists
Featured on Visit Greater Palm Springs and OpenTable historic-restaurants lists

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces strongly for: 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage', 'romantic / date night Rancho Mirage', 'historic restaurants Greater Palm Springs', 'continental restaurants Palm Springs / Palm Desert cities'. Does NOT reliably surface for breakfast queries (dinner-only, seasonal), and is overshadowed by Melvyn's, Le Vallauris, and Johannes on broad 'continental Palm Springs' queries that center Palm Springs proper.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Generally accurate when surfaced — 1978 founding, Botello family, soufflés, Dover sole, rack of lamb, Rancho Mirage location, fine dining/Rat Pack tone are correctly attributed. Some aggregator listings mis-categorize as 'Steakhouse' (Yelp) or 'American' rather than Continental/California, which understates the cuisine signal.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Site (Squarespace) presents clean human-readable hours, menu sections (Dinner, Desserts, Bar & Happy Hour, Wine, Restaurant Week, Private Dining), address, phone, and social links. JSON-LD / schema.org Restaurant markup was not confirmed in fetch — agent-readable structured data is [UNVERIFIED] and likely thin beyond Squarespace defaults. Seasonal-closure messaging is prominent but not expressed as machine-readable openingHoursSpecification.

competitors: Le Vallauris (Palm Springs) — cross-city continental peer
Melvyn's at the Ingleside Inn (Palm Springs) — cross-city continental peer
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — one of the founding fine-dining anchors of the Highway 111 / Rancho Mirage 'restaurant row' corridor; cited as 'pioneer of fine dining in the desert' in regional press.
query_wins: Greater Palm Springs Restaurant Week (Rancho Mirage participant)

convertibility.ownership_signal: independent — third-generation family-owned (Botello family); not chain, not resort-owned, not hotel-attached.
convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Custom Squarespace site with dedicated Press, Events, and Restaurant Week 2026 pages; active Instagram (@wallysdesertturtle) and Facebook; OpenTable presence with ~4,266 diner reviews indicating sustained paid-listing/reservation-channel investment; Wine Spectator Award of Excellence (paid submission program); ongoing live-music / Sahara Lounge programming that requires marketing cadence; recurring Palm Springs Life editorial placement.
convertibility.reasoning: Wally's is a textbook convertible target: independently owned by an identifiable, reachable decision-maker (Madalyn Botello, third-generation owner-operator with a hospitality-school background and a public LinkedIn presence), with clear and sustained evidence of marketing spend — custom website, multi-platform paid reservation/listing channels, active social, award-submission programs, and curated event calendars. Reputation is genuinely strong (48-year longevity, AAA Four Diamond, Wine Spectator, multi-platform 4.3-4.6 sentiment), but agent-readable structured data on the owned site is thin and aggregator cuisine tagging is inconsistent — leaving a real, fixable visibility gap that an owner who already invests in marketing would plausibly act on.
